desc: Basic matrix operations, transpose properties, symmetric and skew-symmetric matrices, nilpotent matrices, and how matrices interact with vectors and row operations.
Matrix invertibility, similarity of matrices, transpose and determinant properties, linear transformations, and solving homogeneous linear systems using rank and row reduction.
Vector space concepts, proving matrices and solution sets form vector spaces, spaces of continuous and differentiable functions, and verifying vector space axioms.
Vector spaces built from span and generators, determining finite generation, linear independence of vectors, matrices, polynomials and functions, and understanding subspace dimension.
Understanding bases, span, and linear independence across vector spaces like matrices, polynomials, and Rn. Finding bases of subspaces and how coordinates change with different bases.
Subspaces, basis existence, dimension inequalities, direct sums, polynomial bases and the dimension formula for W + W'.
